lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I want you to find a comfortable position. Sitting or lying. It doesn't matter anymore.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Four count. [pause] Hold.
Release through your mouth. [pause] There's no rush now.
The hard hours are behind us. [pause] The hardest hours. [pause] You made it through.
Breathe in. [pause] This time, as you breathe, I want you to remember something.
Not something from tonight. [pause] Something from before.
A year ago. [pause] Maybe two years. Maybe longer.
A Purge night you remember. [pause] One where someone you knew didn't make it home.
Breathe. [pause] You don't have to name them. The Sisters know the names.
The names are written. [pause] Not here. [pause] Somewhere quieter.
Let your shoulders drop. [pause] That weight you're carrying — it doesn't live there anymore.
It lives somewhere else now. [pause] It has a home.
Breathe in. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
Release. [pause] And as you release, I want you to think about light.
Not the sun. [pause] Not yet. It's still dark.
The light under a door. [pause] The light from a window you closed.
The light you chose not to open to.
That was strength. [pause] Do you understand? [pause] That was power.
Breathe. [pause] Your body knows what you did. Your body is proud.
Can you feel that? [pause] The quiet pride of staying still.
Scan down from your crown. [pause] Your scalp. Your temples. Tension.
Release it. [pause] It belongs to someone else.
Your neck. [pause] Your shoulders. [pause] Breathe into the tightness.
Your chest. [pause] Is it tight? [pause] Most people's chests are tight tonight.
Breathe into it. [pause] Don't fight it. [pause] Invite it to leave slowly.
Your stomach. [pause] Your hips. [pause] ka-something-deh-venna.
Your legs. [pause] Your feet. [pause] The ones outside cannot touch your feet.
You are safe in this room. [pause] Or this car. [pause] Or this basement.
Wherever you are, you chose it. [pause] That choice protects you.
Breathe in. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release for four.
There is someone you have not forgiven. [pause] You know who they are.
Not forgiven for something they did on a Purge night. [pause] On a night like this.
I'm not asking you to forgive them now. [pause] I'm asking you to set them down.
They are not your responsibility. [pause] They never were.
Breathe. [pause] Imagine them — whoever they are — imagine them outside.
In the dark. [pause] In the cold. [pause] In the consequence of their own choices.
That is not your fault. [pause] Repeat: not your fault.
The ones outside made their choices. [pause] You made yours.
You chose the light of a closed door. [pause] You chose silence.
Breathe in. [pause] Four. [pause] As you hold, I want you to see the sun.
Not here. Not yet. [pause] But coming.
The sun doesn't ask permission. [pause] It doesn't care what happened in the dark.
It rises anyway. [pause] And you will rise with it.
Release. [pause] Breathe out all the names you've been holding.
Breathe out all the doors you didn't open.
Breathe out the sound of the clock. {{CLOCK}}. [pause] Still here.
Still safe in the dark. [pause] Still choosing light.
When you're ready — and there's no rush — open your eyes.
Welcome back to the broadcast. [pause] You made it.

tickerItems.text: The Sisters know the names. Not here. Somewhere quieter.
If you are experiencing intrusive thoughts, SafeCall line remains active: 608-SAFE-247. Trained counselors standing by.
You chose the light of a closed door. You chose silence. That is strength.
East Side activity remains elevated through {{ELAPSED}}. North Side holding steady. Beltline corridor reports decreased vehicle incidents post-01:30.
